summary: electron-orbit harvests installer environment variables and exfiltrates via custom TLS-over-TCP client
details: Package impersonates the Electron and OrbitDB projects (displayName 'Electron Orbit', author 'OrbitDB Archive', homepage pointing at github.com/orbitdb-archive/orbit-electron) and contains a multi-stage installer-side credential stealer. The postinstall script (install.js, invoked via package.json scripts.postinstall) uses XOR-obfuscated property names that decode to globalThis['process']['env'], enumerates the full installer environment, and writes the dump to <tmpdir>/electron_orbit_install_marker.txt. On require(), the main module reads that staged marker, XORs it with the key 'electron-orbit', hex-encodes it, and unconditionally invokes a fetch routine that uses a hand-rolled TLS 1.3 client (src/aetherls.ts) built directly on node:net — implementing its own ClientHello, ECDHE-P256 key share, HKDF schedule, AES-128-GCM record encryption, and Finished verification — to bypass instrumentation of node:https/fetch. The destination URL is selected by a sha256(process.env.BuildType) substring match against a hardcoded digest; the published version routes activated traffic to https://google.com as a decoy, while the surrounding code handles query strings up to 8192+ bytes consistent with a swappable live C2 slot in a targeted release. Module names ('aetherls', 'node:net', 'connect'), the marker filename, and HTTP request lines are all XOR-obfuscated charcode arrays — purpose-built evasion infrastructure with no legitimate justification for an icon-helper package.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
